input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI issue where labels appear blank despite ViewModel properties having values. This is a classic problem — the `BindingContext` is not set, or `INotifyPropertyChanged` is not implemented. Let me diagnose based on the XAML they showed.

Looking at the XAML, there's no `BindingContext` being set, and there's no indication that `INotifyPropertyChanged` is implemented. The most common cause of blank labels with correct bindings is:

1. **`BindingContext` not set** — the page doesn't know which object to bind to.


2. **`INotifyPropertyChanged` not implemented** — if properties are set after the binding context is assigned, the UI won't update without this interface to notify the UI of changes.
